title: Python loops vs NumPy — the 200x gap explained
description:
Your Python loop is 200x slower than it has to be.
One NumPy line. Same result. Here's why it matters.
#Shorts #Python #NumPy #DataScience
hook_visual: Screen recording: Python loop running with visible cursor delay and a '12.4s' timer counting up — hard cut to a single NumPy line executing with '0.08s' displayed. Text overlay in first 2 seconds: '200x slower'
end_screen_cta: Full NumPy tutorial on the channel — link above. Which part clicked last for you: vectorization or the axis parameter?
